signals.reasoning: BTC is rebounding, but macro remains RED and the edge is cleaner in selective alts than in the major itself. Neutral is still the disciplined baseline.
ETH is participating in the bounce, but not with enough relative strength to justify upgrading it over the better alt setups. Best left neutral.
SOL still reads more like a short-covering reset than a durable breakout. With macro still weak, it is not clean enough to promote.
CHILLGUY is constructive, but the move is already extended and the funding edge is no longer clean. It looks more like a watchlist runner than a fresh long entry.
VVV is bouncing, but the move still looks more like a short-covering rally than a clean durable trend continuation. Not enough edge to call a fresh trade.

WLD remains the cleanest actual long on the board. Price and open interest are still rising together, the broader structure remains upward, and it continues to show real relative strength rather than just a squeeze spike.
WCT is improving, but it still looks more like a recovery from prior weakness than a high-quality fresh breakout. Better left as a watch-only rebound.
MON is constructive enough to avoid shorting, but not strong enough to upgrade. It is a middling trend-supported recovery rather than a standout setup.
AIXBT is still too thin and too neutral to offer a meaningful edge. No reason to force it.
STABLE remains the strongest raw squeeze long. Funding is still clearly negative while price stays firm, so it keeps a reduced-size long upgrade even with the weaker macro regime.
GOAT remains damaged, but the downside is already too mature and unstable to trust as a fresh short. Weak structure alone is not enough.
